blocks.content: Her parents first — her mother holding Cassandra's hand in both of hers and not letting go for a long time. Her father stood at the foot of the bed and said very little, which was its own kind of language. Cassandra said I'm okay, Dad, I promise, and he nodded and looked at the window.

She came alone. She stood in the doorway for a moment and looked at her sister — really looked, the way she didn't usually let herself look at the truth of Cassandra's condition. The thing she had loved her sister around for years without letting herself face it directly.
The hospital bed. The line in her arm. The monitor. Her sister in the middle of all of it, alive, looking back at her.
Maya's jaw worked. She crossed the room. Sat on the edge of the bed and took Cassandra's hand and said nothing for a long time.
Maya, who always had something to say. Who had weaponized wit against vulnerability her entire life. Who had spent years making sure no feeling ever arrived without a punchline.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I kept thinking about the drive here. Nathan was driving and I couldn't stop thinking — I never told her. All of it. I kept telling myself she knows, she doesn't need to hear it, we don't say things like that.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You don't get to tell me you know this time. I'm saying it.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You are the only person in the world who has always known exactly who I am. Not who I perform. Not who I pretend to be on the days I can't manage anything else. Who I actually am. You have known me since I was eight years old and you have never once made me feel like that was a burden.
Her voice was holding on by the thinnest thread.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I know I'm not easy. I know I make everything a joke because the alternative is feeling it and feeling things is terrifying. You have known all of that and you have loved me anyway. Every single time. And I have never once told you what that means to me.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You're not as complicated as you think.
Maya laughed. One sound. It broke in the middle.
They held hands. Both of them crying and neither one pretending otherwise.
A breath. A broken laugh. A held hand.
